summary: Matched a group of tournament-mode gmtou functions and refined the surrounding type/header surface needed for those matches. The PR implemented several previously stubbed functions in src/melee/gm/gmtou.c, especially menu/JObj update callbacks and tournament game-cache setup helpers, while expanding TmData, adding TmVsData, moving CSSIconHud into mn/forward.h, and renaming tournament MinorSceneKind values to better describe setup/bracket/alternate winner-out or loser-out flows.
what_changed: Implemented several gmtou.c tournament-mode callbacks that were previously left as function stubs, including fn_8019C3EC, fn_8019C570, fn_8019CA38, fn_8019CC74, fn_8019CDBC, fn_8019D074, fn_8019EE80, and fn_8019EF08.

Updated gmtou.c include dependencies from older local-style includes toward specific sysdolphin and melee headers, adding dependencies such as controller, gm_18A5.h, lbdvd.h, lb/types.h, mnmain.h, and sc/types.h.

Refined tournament JObj/menu behavior: callbacks now hide or show JObjs based on HSD_PadMasterStatus controller errors, position UI objects based on tournament player count, update animation frames with fn_8019044C, and use fn_8018FDC4 for transform placement.
Added tournament game-cache helpers fn_8019EE80 and fn_8019EF08 that write TmVsData character, color, and stage information into lbDvd_8001822C()->game_cache, then flush/update via lbDvd_80018254.

Expanded struct TmData in gm/types.h with named byte fields around x2C-x33, tournament menu data at x37[16], selection data at x4B8[4], extra text pointer arrays including x534[3], and padding to preserve offsets.
Added struct TmVsData with stage_id, slot_type[4], char_id[4], and color[4], and forward-declared TmVsData in gm/forward.h.
Changed TmAnimTimers in gmtou.static.h by replacing x6/x8 with x6[2] and expanding x20 into a four-player array of small per-player state structs, documented as likely JObj states.
Added gm_8019ECAC_OnEnter_t in gmtou.static.h and externs for lbl_804D6690 and lbl_804D6694 SceneDesc pointers.

Renamed tournament minor scene enum values from MN_TOU, MN_TOU_BRACKET, and MN_TOU_FINISH to MN_TOU_SETUP, MN_TOU_BRACKET, and MN_TOU_ALT, with comments describing setup, bracket match type, and winner-out/loser-out alternate match type.
Updated scene tables in gm_1B03.c and gmscdata.c to use MN_TOU_SETUP and MN_TOU_ALT after the enum rename.
Moved or exposed CSSIconHud through src/melee/mn/forward.h instead of requiring mn/types.h from gm_18A5.h, following review feedback to avoid including types.h from headers.

Refined gm_18A5 prototypes and implementations: fn_8018F62C now returns u32 from gobj->user_data, fn_8018F6FC takes CSSIconHud and returns CharacterKind, fn_8018FBE0 received concrete parameters, and fn_80196564/fn_80196594 now return bool and take TmData*.
Refactored lb/types.h PreloadCacheScene layout to contain a nested GameCache struct with major_id, stage_id, and entries, and changed PreloadCacheSceneEntry fields to char_id and color.
smart_moves: The PR matched gmtou functions by first improving the surrounding type information: TmData, TmVsData, GameCache, CSSIconHud, and several gm_18A5 prototypes were made more precise so tournament callbacks could be expressed in typed C instead of placeholder-heavy code.
Moving CSSIconHud to mn/forward.h solved the immediate header dependency problem noted in review while preserving type safety for gm_18A5.h prototypes.

Renaming the tournament minor scene values to MN_TOU_SETUP and MN_TOU_ALT made the scene table entries more semantically searchable and avoided misleading names such as MN_TOU_FINISH for the alternate winner-out/loser-out match type.
The implementation preserved offset-sensitive struct work with explicit padding and named fields only where there was evidence from matched code, rather than overclaiming complete struct knowledge.

The gmtou callbacks reused existing engine helpers such as GET_JOBJ, HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll, HSD_JObjClearFlagsAll, HSD_JObjGetChild, HSD_JObjGetNext, fn_8018FDC4, fn_8019044C, HSD_SisLib_803A746C, and HSD_PadMasterStatus instead of inventing tournament-specific abstractions.
The game-cache struct reshaping in lb/types.h gave fn_8019EE80/fn_8019EF08 natural field accesses for stage_id, char_id, and color, which likely helped matching and readability at the same time.
decomp_lessons: For menu/tournament matching, typed access to persistent mode data such as TmData can unlock multiple callbacks at once; even partial field naming around known offsets is useful when the same offsets recur across functions.
If a header only needs an enum or opaque type, prefer moving that lightweight declaration to a forward.h header rather than including a broad types.h from public headers.
Scene enum names should track observed behavior in scene tables and comments; fixing enum names early improves every handler table and makes future PRs easier to grep.
GObj user_data can encode small integer indices in some menu systems. In this PR, fn_8018F62C documents that tournament uses user_data as an int controlling menu JObj state, and returns it as u32.
When matching UI code, controller status and visibility checks often come before transform/frame updates; preserving this order and using engine flag helpers directly can be important for a match.

Do not force null-safe helper-looking code when the compiler wants direct chained access. fn_8019CFA4 includes a note that the matching form used chained HSD_JObjGetChild/HSD_JObjGetNext calls and that the inline situation seemed uncertain.

Use comments cautiously for uncertain discoveries. This PR marks several fields and behaviors with question marks or tentative comments, such as tournament CSS icon frame behavior and per-player JObj states.
Nested struct extraction can improve matching when code naturally accesses grouped data, as with PreloadCacheScene.game_cache and entries[i].char_id/color.
naming_conventions: Tournament minor scenes were renamed with explicit MN_TOU_* names: MN_TOU_SETUP for selections/settings, MN_TOU_BRACKET for tournament match type, and MN_TOU_ALT for winner-out/loser-out match type.

CSSIconHud enum values use ICONHUD_* names and preserve in-game/icon ordering, including ICONHUD_CAPTAIN, ICONHUD_DONKEY, ICONHUD_FOX, ICONHUD_GAMEWATCH, ICONHUD_KOOPA, ICONHUD_MARS, ICONHUD_POPONANA, ICONHUD_PURIN, ICONHUD_CLINK, ICONHUD_DRMARIO, ICONHUD_EMBLEM, and ICONHUD_GANON.
The PR kept unknown tournament fields offset-based, such as x37, x4B8, x534, and TmUnkMenuData fields, instead of inventing descriptive names without strong evidence.
Preload cache fields were renamed from placeholder field names to behavior-derived names: char_id, color, stage_id, and game_cache.
Function names remained address-based placeholders such as fn_8019C3EC and fn_8019EE80 where no real symbol name was established.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used explicit casts around indices and enum conversions, such as casting gobj->user_data to u32 and returning CharacterKind from CSSIconHud conversion, to make prototypes and generated code line up.
Represented player-count-dependent positioning with repeated float formulas using constants 13.0f, -19.5f, and 6.5f, matching tournament layout behavior for 2, 3, or 4 entrants.
Used sentinel transform values such as 666.0f and 0.01f in fn_8018FDC4 calls, matching existing menu transform conventions rather than abstracting them away.
Kept exact loop bounds where behavior differed: fn_8019EE80 loops to gm_8018F634()->x30, while fn_8019EF08 loops over four slots and skips slot_type value 3.
Used HSD_ASSERT in fn_8019CDBC before dereferencing a JObj, preserving expected assertion behavior at source line 993.

In fn_8019CFA4, chained HSD_JObjGetNext(HSD_JObjGetNext(HSD_JObjGetChild(GET_JOBJ(gobj)))) replaced a more verbose child/next/null-check sequence, with a todo noting uncertainty about a different inline.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Asked to avoid including types.h from headers and suggested moving CSSIconHud to a forward.h header.

review_feedback.author: sadkellz
review_feedback.feedback: Acknowledged the header feedback with 'o7'. The diff excerpt shows gm_18A5.h including melee/mn/forward.h rather than melee/mn/types.h, consistent with the requested direction.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename TmData x37, x4B8, x534, or TmUnkMenuData fields after additional tournament-mode matches.
Search for future cleanup of the fn_8019CFA4 todo about a cursed inline or chained HSD_JObjGetChild/HSD_JObjGetNext access.
Look for PRs that further refine TmVsData, GameCache, or PreloadCacheScene after fn_8019EE80 and fn_8019EF08.
Search for subsequent tournament scene enum usage of MN_TOU_SETUP, MN_TOU_BRACKET, and MN_TOU_ALT.
Find related PRs touching CSSIconHud in mn/forward.h or converting CSS icon IDs to CharacterKind.
